front: How is age corrected for prematurity?
back: Subtract the weeks of prematurity from chronological age (e.g., born at 34 weeks = 6 weeks preterm -> subtract 6 weeks) [source brief]

back: Bayley, Stanford-Binet, Wechsler Intelligence, Vineland adaptive

subject: Pediatrics
front: Newborn RDS is due to deficiency of which surfactant component (lecithin / sphingomyelin / etc.)?
back: Lecithin (phosphatidylcholine) - the main surfactant lipid is dipalmitoyl phosphatidylcholine

subject: Pediatrics
front: 8 yr/26 kg child, severe dehydration, RBS 550, pH 7.01, ketones +ve (DKA): initial management per ISPAD?
back: Manage ABC, NS 20 mL/kg bolus, and start insulin after 1 hour (insulin delayed ~1 hr to reduce cerebral edema risk)

subject: Pediatrics
front: Which finding indicates ADEQUATE growth in an infant (birth weight 3 kg)?
back: Increase in length of 25 cm in the first year (50 cm at birth -> 75 cm at 1 year)

subject: Pediatrics
front: Neonate: post-feed vomiting, hypoglycemia, urine reducing substances +ve (no glucose), reversible oil-drop cataract - diagnosis?

subject: Pediatrics
front: Which belong to NADA's MINOR criteria: (1) systolic murmur grade 3, (2) diastolic murmur, (3) abnormal S2, (4) abnormal BP?
back: 3 and 4 (abnormal S2 and abnormal BP); grade-3 systolic murmur and diastolic murmur are MAJOR criteria

subject: Pediatrics
front: Baby had fever >40 C and inconsolable cry after 6-week DPT; action at 10 weeks?
back: Give DPT vaccine - high fever and persistent crying are precautions, not absolute contraindications

subject: Pediatrics
front: Meconium-stained fluid - which NRP actions are correct: (3) gentle suction in vigorous baby, (4) PPV in non-vigorous baby after initial steps?
back: 3 and 4 only - routine intrapartum suction and routine intratracheal suction are no longer recommended

subject: Pediatrics
front: Which hormone does NOT play a major role in FETAL growth (GH / IGF / thyroxine)?
back: Growth hormone (fetal growth depends on insulin and IGFs, not GH)

subject: Pediatrics
front: Which is NOT a feature of Kawasaki disease (hand/foot edema, thrombocytosis, fever+rash, purulent conjunctivitis)?

subject: Pediatrics
front: Downe score: cyanosis relieved by O2, mild-moderate retractions, grunting on naked ear, markedly decreased air entry, RR 40 - total?
back: Approx 6 (RR<60=0, cyanosis relieved by O2=1, retraction ~1, grunting on naked ear=2, air entry decreased ~2) [retraction & air-entry wording is borderline]

subject: Pediatrics
front: Which statement is FALSE about febrile seizures (age 6 mo-5 yr / simple <15 min / no long-term AEDs / 54% recurrence)?
back: '54% recurrence' is false - recurrence is about 30-35%

front: Which statements are TRUE about CMV: (1) asymptomatic-at-birth have lesser sequelae risk, (3) transmission rate higher from primary maternal infection?
back: 1 and 3 (asymptomatic = lower sequelae risk; vertical transmission rate is higher with primary infection) [no answer key in source; debatable]

subject: Pediatrics
front: TEF anatomical types A-E?
back: A = pure esophageal atresia (no fistula); B = EA + proximal fistula; C = EA + distal fistula (most common); D = EA + both proximal & distal fistulae; E = TEF without atresia (H-type)

subject: Pediatrics
front: Additional clinical features of kwashiorkor (image findings)?
back: Protuberant belly, xerosis, itchy rash, poor wound healing

subject: Pediatrics
front: William syndrome - facial (elfin facies) features?
back: Large ears, full cheeks, small jaw and teeth, upturned nose, wide mouth, epicanthal folds

subject: Pediatrics
front: Approximate testicular volume (orchidometer) by Tanner stage 1-5?
back: ~3, 4, 10, 16, 25 ml for stages 1->5 [from slide's left column; values approximate]

subject: Pediatrics
front: Name the four tissue growth patterns (Scammon curves) and their behavior.
back: Lymphoid (overshoots to ~190-200% then regresses), neural/brain (rapid early, near-complete by 6-7 yr), somatic/general (sigmoid), gonadal (minimal until puberty then rapid)

subject: Pediatrics
front: IMNCI - pneumonia classification by oxygen saturation and chest indrawing?
back: Pneumonia = fast breathing, with or without chest indrawing, and O2 saturation >92%

subject: Pediatrics
front: Neonatal hypothyroidism: USG finding, and what detects dysgenesis?
back: USG may be normal; RAIU (not USG) detects dysgenesis (negative uptake)
